items.title: India’s textile sector at an inflection point as global tariff barriers ease; Arvind Fashion, Raymond Lifestyle top buys
items.description: The immediate reduction in US tariffs on Indian textile goods from 50% to 18% has brought long-awaited relief to an industry that had been grappling with margin compression, regulatory costs, and weakening demand—particularly in home textiles, where the market had declined by around 4% and smaller players were nearing shutdown.

items.title: From 1929 to today: what history tells us about America’s speculative culture and India’s prudence
items.description: The American financial system is structurally designed to accommodate, and often actively encourage, speculative behaviour. Indian markets, by contrast, are deliberately constructed to restrain it.

items.title: Silver ETFs crash 38% in 7 days. Time to buy for those who missed the rally?
items.description: Silver exchange-traded funds (ETFs) have plunged about 38% from their record high reached just seven trading sessions earlier on January 29, as higher margin requirements and profit-booking led to a sharp unwinding of leveraged bets.
